Federal Retirement Checklist | Capital Wealth

Complete federal retirement preparation checklist. Timeline from 5 years out to retirement day covering FERS, TSP, FEHB, FEGLI, and all essential federal employee retirement steps.

Federal Retirement Checklist

Your Federal Retirement

Checklist.

Federal retirement involves complex coordination of FERS, TSP, FEHB, FEGLI, and Social Security. This comprehensive timeline ensures you don't miss critical steps or deadlines that could cost you thousands in benefits.

View Complete Checklist

"The difference between a good retirement and a great one is preparation."

Mike Stevens, IAR
Complete Timeline

Federal Retirement Preparation Timeline

5 Years Before

Strategic Planning Phase

Foundation & Strategy

  • Calculate Your FERS Projection Use our calculator or get professional analysis to understand your pension amount and identify optimization opportunities.
  • Verify FEHB Eligibility Ensure continuous enrollment for 5 years before retirement. This is critical — you cannot get FEHB back if you don't meet the requirement.
  • Review Military Buyback Options If you have military service, analyze whether buying back time makes financial sense. This requires a 3% deposit plus interest if past 3 years of service.
  • Maximize TSP Contributions Ensure you're at least getting full agency match (5%). Consider increasing contributions, especially if you're behind on retirement savings.
  • Analyze FEGLI Coverage Review your life insurance needs and costs. Option B can become extremely expensive in retirement — explore private alternatives now.

2 Years Before

Optimization Phase

Benefit Optimization

  • Optimize Your High-3 Salary Work toward promotions or step increases to maximize your highest 3-year average. Every dollar increase adds $10-11 annually to your pension.
  • Plan Social Security Strategy Understand how FERS supplement coordinates with Social Security. Plan claiming strategy considering WEP/GPO elimination benefits.
  • Review TSP Allocation Adjust investment strategy for your changing time horizon. Consider if you want to keep TSP after retirement or roll to IRA.
  • Estate Planning Update Review beneficiaries on all accounts, update wills, consider survivor benefit elections. Coordinate FERS survivor benefits with other assets.
  • Healthcare Transition Planning Research FEHB plans for retirees, understand Medicare coordination at 65, plan for any needed medical care before retirement.

1 Year Before

Implementation Phase

Final Preparations

  • Attend Retirement Seminar Participate in your agency's retirement workshop or attend our federal benefits seminar for latest updates and procedures.
  • Accumulate Sick Leave Minimize sick leave usage to maximize service credit. All unused sick leave adds to your service time for annuity calculation.
  • Final FEGLI Decision Make final decision on life insurance continuation. If switching to private insurance, secure coverage before canceling FEGLI.
  • Tax Planning Consider Roth conversions in lower-income years, plan for required minimum distributions, understand tax impact of retirement income sources.
  • Budget for Retirement Create detailed retirement budget including all income sources (FERS, TSP, Social Security) and expenses including healthcare costs.

6 Months Before

Application Phase

Formal Application Process

  • Submit Retirement Application File SF-3107 (FERS) or SF-2801 (CSRS) with your HR office. 90 days advance notice is recommended, 60 days minimum.
  • Complete Beneficiary Forms Submit SF-3102 (FERS) designation of beneficiary and update TSP beneficiaries. Ensure all forms are current and accurate.
  • Plan TSP Withdrawal Decide on TSP withdrawal strategy: monthly payments, partial lump sum, annuity, or combination. Submit appropriate forms.
  • FEHB Election Confirm FEHB plan selection for retirement. You can change plans during open season but cannot re-enroll if you drop coverage.
  • Survivor Benefit Decision Make final decision on FERS survivor benefit election. This is irrevocable — choose full, partial, or no survivor benefit carefully.

Final Month

Transition Phase

Final Steps & Coordination

  • Verify All Applications Confirm HR received all retirement paperwork, verify effective date, ensure no missing forms or signatures.
  • Coordinate Direct Deposit Set up or verify banking information for retirement annuity payments, TSP distributions, and Social Security (if applicable).
  • Plan Gap Coverage Understand there may be a gap between final paycheck and first retirement payment. Plan cash flow accordingly.
  • Healthcare Transition Understand when FEHB coverage transitions, get necessary prescriptions filled, schedule any needed medical appointments.
  • Update Address & Contact Info Ensure OPM has current address for retirement communications, update contact info with TSP and Social Security Administration.

Retirement Day

Celebration & Next Steps

Welcome to Retirement!

  • Monitor First Payments Watch for interim payments from OPM (may take 30-60 days for first full payment). TSP distributions should begin as scheduled.
  • Review Annual Statements Verify all retirement benefit amounts are correct on first statements. Contact appropriate agencies immediately if you find errors.
  • Plan Social Security Timing If eligible for FERS supplement, it should begin automatically. Plan Social Security claiming strategy for age 62+ based on your situation.
  • Annual Benefit Reviews Schedule annual review of retirement income, tax situation, healthcare needs, and investment performance to stay on track.
  • Enjoy Your Retirement! You've earned it through years of federal service. Take time to enjoy the security and benefits of your well-planned retirement.
Common Questions

Retirement Planning FAQ

Essential information about federal retirement preparation, timing, and procedures for a smooth transition.

Get Personal Guidance

Ready to plan your federal retirement?

This checklist covers the essentials, but every federal employee's situation is unique. Schedule a comprehensive review to create your personalized federal retirement strategy and ensure you don't miss any opportunities.
